title: Contributing to submitr
text: submitr wraps system commands (ssh, scp, condor_submit, condor_q) tested end--end without SSH access CHTC submit node live HTCondor environment. test suite addresses three-layer strategy. Layer 1 – Argument validation. Pure R checks bad arguments error correctly required arguments enforced. tests run every platform, including CRAN, external dependencies. Layer 2 – Command construction. Tests correct system command assembled supplied arguments. dry_run = TRUE causes functions print command without running . Mocked bindings intercept internal system checks tests run without SSH, HTCondor, live submit node. Layer 3 – Integration. Tests call real system commands live CHTC environment. tests guarded CHTC_USERNAME environment variable. run CHTC_USERNAME set non-empty value – CHTC NetID. never run CRAN CI variable unset. run Layer 3 integration tests locally, set CHTC NetID running test suite: Unset afterward return default skip behavior: running integration tests, confirm : SSH access CHTC submit node ap2002.chtc.wisc.edu; ControlMaster configured ~/.ssh/config avoid repeated Duo MFA prompts (see htc_config() documentation setup guidance); htc_config() can connect submit node without error.
code: Sys.setenv(CHTC_USERNAME = "your.netid") devtools::test() Sys.unsetenv("CHTC_USERNAME")

title: NA
text: Error 1 – absolute paths Dockerfile COPY instructions. containr::generate_dockerfile() wrote absolute host paths (/Users/lares/Desktop/...) COPY instructions .validate_file_arg() returned normalizePath() output. Podman resolve paths build context. Fix: .validate_file_arg() now returns paths relative getwd() via fs::path_rel(). Files outside build context error immediately. generate_dockerfile() COPY blocks simplified glue::glue("COPY {.x} /home/{.x}"). Error 2 – flattened directory structure container. COPY destinations used basename(), data-raw/sample.csv ended /home/data/sample.csv instead /home/data-raw/sample.csv. R script’s relative paths broke inside container. Fix: COPY destinations now mirror source path /home/. Local directory structure preserved. Error 3 – missing docker:// prefix submit file. htc_gen_submit() wrote container_image verbatim. Without docker:// prefix, HTCondor treated image path local file submit node errored “file directory.” Fix: htc_gen_submit() now prepends docker:// automatically prefix missing. Error 4 – missing transfer directives submit file. generated .sub file lacked should_transfer_files = YES when_to_transfer_output = ON_EXIT. Without , HTCondor’s file transfer mechanism activate. Fix: directives now always included transfer section. Error 5 – arm64 container x86_64 cluster. container built Apple Silicon Mac, producing arm64 image. CHTC execute nodes x86_64 rejected image “Image Architecture arm64 compatible machine.” Building --platform linux/amd64 via Podman failed due QEMU emulation segfaults. Docker Desktop’s buildx handled cross-platform build successfully. Fix: containr::build_image() gained platform parameter defaulting "linux/amd64". Docker tool target platform differs host, function automatically uses docker buildx build --load. Error 6 – HTCondor working directory mismatch. HTCondor starts executable scratch directory, container’s WORKDIR. generated .sh script used relative paths (Rscript ntbk2clstr.R) resolved scratch directory files exist. Fix: htc_gen_executable() now emits cd /home file operations, ensuring paths resolve container’s baked-file layout. Error 7 – silent failures executable script. generated .sh script lacked set -euo pipefail. Failed commands (e.g. missing file) stop script, making debugging harder. Fix: htc_gen_executable() now emits set -euo pipefail immediately shebang line.

title: submitr – Package Development Plan
text: submitr provides scaffolding tools help researchers prepare submit computational jobs high-throughput computing (HTC) schedulers. package guides users generating files required run containerized R analyses HTCondor, including submit files executable scripts, wraps system commands needed upload files, submit jobs, monitor status, retrieve results. package explicitly HTC-first. HTCondor CHTC initial targets. HPC scheduler support (Slurm, PBS) planned future release. design mission scaffolding guidance, just wrapping system commands. Every function written help researcher understand — comments argument generator functions ControlMaster guidance htc_config() reflect directly. full workflow submitr supports:
code: toolero::write_by_group()       -- split dataset into subsets + manifest toolero::create_qmd()           -- scaffold analysis.qmd knitr::purl()                   -- strip analysis.qmd to analysis.R containr::generate_dockerfile() -- generate Dockerfile containr::build_image()         -- build container image locally containr::list_images()         -- inspect local images, find image ID containr::push_image()          -- push container to registry submitr::htc_config()           -- configure HTC connection (htc.cfg) submitr::htc_gen_submit()       -- generate .sub file submitr::htc_gen_executable()   -- generate .sh file submitr::htc_upload()           -- copy files to CHTC submit node via scp submitr::htc_submit()           -- run condor_submit, returns cluster ID submitr::htc_status()           -- monitor via condor_q, optional watch loop submitr::htc_download()         -- retrieve results via scp, glob support

title: Generate an HTCondor executable shell script for an R job — htc_gen_executable
text: output_file character string. Name shell script write. Must end ".sh". Defaults "job.sh". r_script character string. Name R script HTCondor run inside container, e.g. "analysis.R". Must supplied explicitly – default. used toolero::create_qmd(use_purl = TRUE), script .R file produced purl.R rendering. results_folder character string. Name folder created inside container hold job outputs compression. Defaults "results-folder". mode character string. Execution mode. "single" (default) runs R script without positional argument, producing single fixed-name results tarball. "multiple" passes subset filename positional argument via ${1}, producing per-job tarball named ${1}-results.tar.gz. Must match mode used htc_gen_submit(). set_executable Logical. TRUE, sets executable permissions generated script via Sys.chmod() file ready copy CHTC submit node without additional steps. Defaults TRUE. Set FALSE prefer manage permissions manually, case must run chmod +x script submitting job. verbose Logical. TRUE, prints progress messages section script written. Defaults FALSE. comments Logical. TRUE, annotates section explanatory comment describing line . Useful researchers learning HTCondor executable script conventions. Defaults FALSE. output character string. Directory shell script written. Defaults "." (current working directory).

Files transfer back job's working directory execution. "multiple" mode, defaults "$(file)-results.tar.gz" supplied. Defaults NULL. mode character string. Submission mode. "single" (default) submits one job. "multiple" submits one job per row manifest supplied queue_from, passing subset file positional argument executable via arguments = $(file). queue positive integer. Number identical jobs submit. used mode = "single". Defaults 1. queue_from character string. Path manifest file produced toolero::write_by_group(manifest = TRUE). Required mode = "multiple". file_path column extracted written alongside submit file subdatasets.csv, HTCondor reads generate one job per subset file. resources character string. Compute resource preset. One "small", "medium", "large", "custom" (requires custom_resources). Default preset values reflect CHTC recommendations loaded inst/extdata/htc-resources.yaml. local htc-resources.yaml working directory takes precedence package default, allowing per-project customization. Defaults "small". custom_resources named list. Required resources = "custom". Must contain cpus (integer), memory (character, e.g. "8GB"), disk (character, e.g. "4GB"). Ignored resources "custom". gpu Logical. TRUE, adds GPU resource requests submit file. Defaults FALSE. gpu_options named list NULL. Fine-grained GPU options applied gpu = TRUE. Supported keys: request_gpus (integer, default 1), want_gpu_lab (logical, default TRUE), min_capability (numeric, e.g. 8.0 A100; NULL omit), min_memory_mb (integer MB, e.g. 40000; NULL omit). gpu = TRUE gpu_options = NULL, CHTC defaults used. verbose Logical. TRUE, prints progress messages section submit file written. Defaults FALSE. comments Logical. TRUE, annotates section explanatory comment describing section use . Defaults FALSE. output character string. Directory submit file (, "multiple" mode, subdatasets.csv) written. Defaults "." (current working directory).

code: # Single-job submit file with default resource preset htc_gen_submit(output = tempdir())  # Single-job submit file with medium resources and file transfer htc_gen_submit(   output_file     = "analysis.sub",   container_image = "docker://registry.doit.wisc.edu/netid/myimage",   executable      = "analysis.sh",   input_files     = "analysis.R",   output_files    = "results.tar.gz",   resources       = "medium",   output          = tempdir() )  # Annotated submit file useful for learning HTCondor syntax htc_gen_submit(   output_file = "annotated.sub",   comments    = TRUE,   verbose     = TRUE,   output      = tempdir() ) #> Writing submit file header #> Writing container section #> Writing executable section #> Writing file transfer section #> Writing logging section #> Writing resources section (small preset: 1 CPU / 4GB RAM / 4GB disk) #> Writing queue section (1 job) #> ✔ Submit file written to /tmp/RtmpZmGxcg/annotated.sub  # Custom resource request htc_gen_submit(   resources        = "custom",   custom_resources = list(cpus = 2, memory = "8GB", disk = "4GB"),   output           = tempdir() )  if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Multiple-job submit file driven by a write_by_group() manifest htc_gen_submit(   output_file     = "analysis.sub",   container_image = "docker://registry.doit.wisc.edu/netid/myimage",   executable      = "analysis.sh",   input_files     = "analysis.R",   mode            = "multiple",   queue_from      = "data/manifest.csv",   resources       = "medium",   output          = "." ) } # }

what: submitr: Scaffold and Submit Computational Jobs to HTC Schedulers — submitr-package
title: submitr: Scaffold and Submit Computational Jobs to HTC Schedulers — submitr-package
text: Provides scaffolding tools help researchers prepare submit computational jobs high-throughput computing (HTC) schedulers. Generates files required run containerized R analyses 'HTCondor', including submit files executable scripts, wraps system commands needed stage files, submit jobs, monitor status, retrieve results CHTC submit node. Provides 'htc_config()' managing connection details SSH connection reuse guidance. Works naturally alongside 'containr' container image management 'toolero' dataset splitting project scaffolding.

title: submitr 0.1.0.9000
text: htc_gen_submit() now prepends docker:// container_image prefix missing. Previously, omitting prefix caused HTCondor treat image path local file. htc_gen_submit() now includes should_transfer_files = YES when_to_transfer_output = ON_EXIT transfer section. directives required HTCondor file transfer mechanism work. htc_gen_executable() now includes set -euo pipefail shebang line, causing script exit immediately errors instead silently continuing. htc_gen_executable() now includes cd /home file operations, ensuring script runs container’s working directory containr::generate_dockerfile() placed baked-files.

title: submitr 0.1.0
text: htc_config() – create read project-level htc.cfg configuration file. first use, prompts interactively username server, displays ControlMaster SSH setup guidance reduce Duo MFA prompts, writes htc.cfg, adds .gitignore. Subsequent calls read existing file validate server reachability. Returns named list username server. Errors informatively username server supplied empty strings.

title: submitr 0.1.0
text: htc_gen_submit() – generate HTCondor .sub submit file project parameters. Supports single-job multiple-job modes. Multiple mode reads manifest toolero::write_by_group(manifest = TRUE), extracts filenames, writes subdatasets.csv, emits queue file subdatasets.csv. Resource presets (small, medium, large, custom) loaded runtime inst/extdata/htc-resources.yaml; local ./htc-resources.yaml takes precedence package default. GPU support via gpu = TRUE gpu_options. comments = TRUE annotates section generated file explanatory text. htc_gen_executable() – generate .sh executable script HTCondor runs inside container. Produces four-element script: shebang, mkdir, Rscript, tar. multiple-job mode, passes ${1} positional argument R script. r_script must supplied explicitly – default. set_executable = TRUE (default) sets executable permissions via Sys.chmod().

title: submitr 0.1.0
text: htc_upload() – copy files CHTC submit node via scp. Accepts single files, vectors files, directories (transferred recursively), glob patterns. remote_path defaults "~/". dry_run = TRUE previews command without executing . htc_submit() – run condor_submit remote submit node via SSH remote directory files uploaded. Returns cluster ID invisibly use htc_status(). Supports dry_run = TRUE. htc_status() – check job progress via condor_q. Optionally filters cluster ID. watch = TRUE polls interval seconds (default 60) cluster ID leaves queue. Returns condor_q output invisibly character vector. Supports dry_run = TRUE. htc_download() – copy result files back submit node via scp. Supports single filenames, vectors filenames, glob patterns ("*.tar.gz", "job.*"). Glob patterns single-quoted prevent local shell expansion. local_path defaults ".". Supports dry_run = TRUE.
